exemplar.title:
How does the relative distance between a kicker and a goalkeeper, during a game of soccer,
affect the range of angles he can shoot to score?

detailedExemplarData.title:
How does the relative distance between a kicker and a goalkeeper, during a game of soccer,
affect the range of angles he can shoot to score?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: The research question is clearly stated and tightly linked to every section, maintaining a strong, consistent focus.
Clear purpose communicated via an explicit table of contents and repeated restatement of aims.
Methodology is well-justified with diagrams, controls, and use of appropriate software tools.
weaknesses: Occasional wording imprecision (e.g. switching between ârange of anglesâ and âangle he can shootâ) slightly blurs clarity.
Minor methodological confusions (misclassification of the independent variable as a control).
Gender-specific phrasing and slight overemphasis on socioeconomic context dilute the scientific focus.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work consistently communicates topic and purpose, formulates a tightly focused question with all variables defined, and presents a mostly complete, justified methodology. Minor imprecision and small conceptual missteps prevent a perfect 6, so a score of 5 reflects highâband performance with room for refinement.

strengths: Solid application of HL mechanics in deriving projectileâmotion relationships and using error propagation.
Correct use of trigonometric identities to decompose velocity components.
Clear algebraic manipulation in deriving the key tangent formula.
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation (using âqâ for gravitational acceleration and missing vector arrows) reduces clarity.
Occasional unit slips and typographical errors in symbols.
Lack of explicit justification for neglecting air resistance or spin limits completeness of physical understanding.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates good subject knowledge and generally accurate terminology, consistently applying theory to data. Symbol misuse, inconsistent notation, and limited discussion of simplifying assumptions prevent topâband performance, placing the work solidly in the 3â4 band with a mark of 4.

strengths: Insightful theoretical modelling and linearisation of variables, with clear relevance to the research question.
Wellâstructured comparison of empirical data versus theoretical predictions, including correlationâcoefficient calculations.
Thoughtful identification of multiple error sources with concrete improvement suggestions.
weaknesses: Statistical treatment remains basic (no regression uncertainties or ÏÂ² analysis).
Some digressions (e.g. economic context) weaken the central argument flow.
Evaluation is largely descriptive, lacking quantitative assessment of how each limitation alters results.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis and evaluation are consistently focused and clearly linked to the question, and the argument is structured from derivation through data critique. While statistical methods are basic and evaluation could be more quantitative, the overall critical thinking meets the top of the 4â6 band, warranting a score of 6.

strengths: Comprehensive title page, accurate word count, and a detailed table of contents enhance navigability.
Consistent heading hierarchy and labelled figures/tables support clear structure.
Embedded diagrams and Tracker screenshots effectively illustrate experimental setup.
weaknesses: Tables sometimes split across pages, breaking table borders and reducing readability.
Figure captions occasionally detach from images, and some visuals are low resolution.
Long codeâstyle strings appear in the main text, distracting from narrative flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report follows EE conventions with clear formatting and effective visual support. Minor layout and readability issues prevent a flawless presentation. Overall, the work merits the top of the 3â4 band, receiving a score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Key termsâprojectile motion, horizontal/vertical components, uncertaintyâare generally used correctly, and equations are presented with proper symbolism.  Nevertheless, inconsistent notation, missing vector arrows, and typographical errors (â$V_{_H}$â, â$\theta_r$â) reduce consistency.  Thus terminology is mostly accurate but not sustained at a consistently high level, aligning with the lower end of the 3-4 band.

A structured argument runs from derivation, through data collection, to evaluation, and the student explicitly revisits the hypothesis.  Coherence is weakened by digressions (economic importance of soccer) and by the final claim that the data âfails to supportâ the model without offering a refined interpretation.  Logical flow is present but not consistently compelling, justifying a moderate score within the band.

The student provides a full set of variables, controls, equipment lists and a multi-step procedure, supported by diagrams and justification of each control.  Use of Tracker software and error-propagation tools shows informed selection of methods.  However, methodological weaknessesâonly three trials, large uncertainties, human kicker variability and camera parallaxâare acknowledged but not resolved, leading to data that ultimately fails to support the theory.  The range of sources is narrow and predominantly web based.  Consequently the methodology is âmostly completeâ but not fully appropriate, meriting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

The derivation of the projectile-motion relationship and the attempt to remove initial velocity demonstrate solid application of HL mechanics.  Calculations of theoretical values, error propagation, and comparison with empirical data show understanding, even if execution is imperfect.  Mis-use of symbols (q for g), occasional unit slips, and very limited treatment of air resistance or spin indicate gaps that prevent the highest band.  Overall, knowledge is âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ.

The evaluation section systematically lists multiple sources of error and proposes concrete improvements (robot kicker, measuring wheel, tripod).  The student comments on uncertainty magnitudes and data-theory mismatch.  Yet the critique remains largely descriptive; there is limited quantitative assessment of how each limitation affects results, and no attempt to refine the model in light of findings.  Hence the evaluation is âgoodâ but not âexcellent.â

The title, abstracted research question and early background sections make the purpose of the work immediately clear: to examine how kickerâgoalkeeper separation influences the available shooting angles.  The student keeps the reader oriented with an explicit table of contents, clear headings, and repeated restatement of the aim throughout the body.  Minor wording awkwardness (e.g. switching between ârange of anglesâ and âangle he can shootâ) very occasionally blurs precision, but the overall communication remains accurate and purposeful, warranting a high-band award, albeit not a perfect one.

Graphs and images are embedded and referenced; Tracker screenshots and schematic diagrams aid comprehension.  Axis labels and units are mostly present, though graph resolution is low and some visuals are pixelated.  Table borders and markdown artefacts occasionally reduce readability, but layout still supports communication better than the basic level, meriting a solid 3.

The question "How does the relative distance between a kicker and a goalkeeper â¦ affect the range of angles he can shoot to score?" is specific, measurable and tightly linked to the subsequent theory and data sections.  All variables (distance, range of angles) are defined and the discussion never strays far from this focus.  Some gender-specific language (âheâ) and the decision to ignore bounces reveal small conceptual imprecision, so a 5 rather than 6 is appropriate.

The student carries out theoretical modelling, collects data, linearises variables and calculates correlation coefficients, demonstrating clear analytical effort.  Strength/limitation tables and comparison of empirical vs theoretical plots show relevance to the question.  However, statistical treatment is basic (no regression uncertainties, no chi-square) and some conclusions rely on qualitative judgement.  This places the work solidly in the middle of the 4-6 band.

The essay includes a title page, word count, contents, consistent heading hierarchy and labelled tables/figures, fulfilling most EE conventions.  Some tables split awkwardly across pages, figure captions are occasionally detached, and long code-style strings appear in the main text, so polish is not perfect.  Overall the structure is clear and functional, earning a mid-band score.

annotations.comment: Consider ensuring that tables do not split awkwardly between pages; broken borders reduce readability. Adjust page breaks or layout.
The rationale focuses heavily on economic and social context rather than physics justification, diluting the scientific purpose. Refocus on physics concepts and relevance.
The background section correctly introduces projectile motion fundamentals and cites sources, establishing necessary theoretical context.
Title accurately reflects the investigationâs focus on kickerâgoalkeeper distance and scoring angles, effectively setting the scope.
Consider using gender-neutral phrasing in the research question (e.g. âtheyâ or âthe playerâ) to ensure inclusive language.
Word count of 3874 meets the requirement and stays within the 4000-word limit.
The comprehensive table of contents provides clear navigation, enhancing the reportâs structure and readability.
Sentence ends abruptly (âThis experiment will test this, byâ), indicating an incomplete thought. Ensure all descriptions are fully articulated.
The symbol âqâ is used for gravitational acceleration; standard convention is âg.â Use consistent, conventional symbols to avoid confusion.
Correct use of trigonometric identities to decompose initial velocity into vertical and horizontal components.
When denoting vector quantities (e.g. velocity components), include arrow notation or bold formatting to clearly distinguish them from scalars.
The substitution for vertical displacement uses \(\frac{gd^2}{V_0^2\sin^2\theta}\) instead of the correct \(\frac{gd^2}{2V_0^2\cos^2\theta}\). Review trigonometric substitutions.
Notation for gravitational acceleration appears as both âgâ and âqâ; choose one symbol and apply it consistently throughout.
Embedding schematic diagrams alongside equations helps visualise the theoretical scenario, aiding conceptual understanding.
Dividing the equations at two distinct points to eliminate initial velocity demonstrates inventive methodology and solid understanding of experimental constraints.
Consider acknowledging and justifying the neglect of air resistance or spin, as these factors can significantly alter projectile trajectories.
The algebraic manipulation to derive \(\tan\theta=\frac{d^2H-D^2h}{d^2D-D^2d}\) is clearly presented, showcasing solid math skills.
The step factoring out \(\tan^2\theta+1\) is mathematically correct but lacks explanatory text; include brief rationale for reader clarity.
Includes distance between kicker and goal as a controlled variable, but this is the independent variable. Reclassify to avoid confusion.
Reduce verbosity in justification column by focusing on essential rationale; concise explanations improve table readability.
Consider including the dependent variable (range of angles) in your controlled variables table to highlight experimental relationships.
The explanation linking parabolic symmetry to angle variation is insightful, demonstrating clear understanding of projectile geometry.
Ensure page numbers are prominent and consistently formatted (e.g. in footer) so readers can easily reference content.
Consider using more distinctive heading styles or larger fonts for section transitions to improve navigation.
